text: The previous chapters revealed how GCC governments have built up their capacities to provide public higher education efficiently. Private higher education has also developed and the increase in the number of public and private higher education institutions has provided a wider range of study opportunities for a larger number of students. Women have benefited in a major way from this expansion, because it has become possible for them to study in the GCC countries. This was not possible in the past for daughters of parents who did not want to send their daughters abroad for study. The expansion of higher education in the GCC countries has also provided a better opportunity for children of expatriate workers to get higher education in the GCC countries they work in, without having to send them back to their home country for study.
headline: Fully Human Written
